title: T-083 — Local UI prototype copy red-lines for Technical final decisions
prompt:
# T-083 — Local UI prototype copy red-lines for Technical final decisions

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §0, §5.1A, §7.4, prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md §0A
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Local copy/guard red-line source for Technical UI tasks, keeping prototypes as visual anchors but correcting production semantics.

This is not app feature implementation. It creates a local copy contract or checklist in allowed PRD/UX/coverage/manifest/tasks scope and task acceptance criteria that implementation agents must follow. Prototype JSX remains an anchor; production UI copy must apply this overlay. Include forbidden legacy terms and allowed exceptions for imported source values/audit history. It complements T-078 rather than depending on it, to avoid a circular implementation dependency.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md [create]
- _meta/atomic-tasks/03-technical/tasks/T-038.json [create]
- _meta/atomic-tasks/03-technical/tasks/T-039.json [create]
- _meta/atomic-tasks/03-technical/tasks/T-060.json [create]
- _meta/atomic-tasks/03-technical/tasks/T-078.json [create]
- _meta/atomic-tasks/03-technical/tasks/T-080.json [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given UI task uses prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/bom-detail.jsx:3-60 or prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:168-243, then structural, visual and interaction parity remains required while production copy applies this red-line overlay.
2. Given production UI copy is reviewed, then FA/PR/D365-first wording is absent except explicit legacy imported-source/audit labels.
3. Given a released BOM/spec edit UI is implemented, then clone-on-write banner copy is present and in-place edit copy is absent.
4. Given RM component selection UI is implemented, then approved supplier, active supplier_spec, allergens, item active, cost/spec review and QC/release checks are listed with typed server codes.
5. Given lab result status appears in Technical UI, then it is labelled Quality-owned read-only and no Technical approval action is shown.
6. UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

## Test strategy
- Add/require UI copy assertions in each referenced UI task closeout.
- Run relevant task tests named in T-038/T-039/T-060/T-078/T-080.
- For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.

## Risk red lines
- Do not edit prototype files for cosmetic churn.
- Do not let prototype legacy labels override PRD §0 decisions.
- Do not make D365 mandatory for local Technical workflows.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim — translate to production shadcn primitives per `_meta/prototype-labels/translation-notes-technical.md`.
- Do not use raw HTML <select> — use shadcn Select.
- Do not use inline styles — use Tailwind classes; conditional via cn()/cva().
- Do not import @radix-ui/* outside packages/ui (ESLint guard).

## Prototype parity
- Evidence policy: `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md` — closeout must include screenshot artifacts + Playwright trace/video where applicable, plus axe results or documented blocker.
